content.problemStatement:
Not everyone probably knows that Chef has younger brother Jeff. Currently Jeff learns to read.

He knows some subset of the letter of Latin alphabet. In order to help Jeff to study, Chef gave him a book with the text consisting of **N** words. Jeff can read a word if it consists only of the letters he knows.

Now Chef is curious about which words his brother will be able to read, and which are not. Please help him!
constraints: Each letter in S appears no more than once.
S and each word consist only of lowercase Latin letters.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a string S of lowercase Latin letters that Jeff knows. The second line contains an integer N, the number of words in the book. Each of the following N lines contains a single word.
content.outputFormat: For each word, output 'Yes' if Jeff can read it, and 'No' otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first word 'cat' can be read because all its characters are in the set of known characters. The second word 'dog' cannot be read because 'd', 'o', and 'g' are not in the set of known characters.
The first word 'abc' can be read. The second word 'abcd' cannot be read because 'd' is not in the set of known characters. The third word 'ab' can be read.

solution.approach: Create a set of characters that Jeff knows. For each word, check if all its characters are in the set of known characters. If they are, output 'Yes'; otherwise, output 'No'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * M) where N is the number of words and M is the maximum length of a word. The set operations (insertion and lookup) are O(1) on average.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(|S| + N * M) for storing the set of known characters and the words.
